channel.title: Beat the Scam
channel.description: Scam alerts, plain-English checks, and practical guides to help people verify suspicious emails, texts, websites, calls, and offers.

item.title: Work from Home Scam UK: How to Spot and Avoid Fake Job Offers
item.description: Work from home scams target UK job seekers with promises of quick money for minimal effort, often using fake job boards and impersonated companies. This guide shows you exactly how these scams operate and what steps to take if you've been contacted.

item.title: WhatsApp Family Message Scam UK: How to Spot and Stop It
item.description: The WhatsApp family message scam tricks you into sending money by impersonating a family member in crisis. Scammers use hacked accounts, stolen contact lists, or spoofed numbers to make the message seem genuine.

item.title: Amazon Scam Email: How to Spot and Avoid Fake Emails in the UK
item.description: Amazon scam emails trick UK customers into clicking malicious links or entering login details on fake websites. Scammers impersonate Amazon to steal passwords, payment information, and personal data by creating urgent-sounding messages about account problems or suspicious activity.

item.title: Charity Scams UK: How to Spot Fake Charities and Protect Your Donation
item.description: Charity scams impersonate real charities or create fake ones to trick donors into sending money that never reaches those in need. Scammers use emotional appeals, fake websites, and convincing emails to steal donations—sometimes thousands of pounds.

item.title: Invoice Redirection Scam: UK Guide to Spotting Fake Payment Requests
item.description: Invoice redirection scams intercept legitimate business communications and redirect payment to fraudster bank accounts. This guide shows you how to spot fake invoices, verify genuine requests, and protect your business payments.

item.title: Recovery Scam UK: How Scammers Pose as Refund Services
item.description: Recovery scams target people who have already lost money to fraud, promising to retrieve their funds for an upfront fee. Scammers impersonate legitimate refund companies, lawyers, or financial investigators to gain trust and extract payment.

item.title: QR Code Scams UK: How to Spot and Avoid Fake Payment Links
item.description: QR code scams are growing in the UK, with criminals replacing legitimate codes or sending fake ones via text, email, or in person. Scanning a malicious QR code can lead to fake payment pages, identity theft, or direct account access.

item.title: QR Code Scam UK: How to Spot Fake Codes and Protect Yourself
item.description: Scammers use fake QR codes in emails, texts, posters, and online adverts to direct you to phishing websites or install malware on your phone. This guide shows you how to recognise these scams and what to do if you've already scanned one.

item.title: Evri Scam Text Messages UK: How to Spot and Report Them
item.description: Scammers send fake Evri text messages claiming your parcel needs attention or payment, then direct you to phishing websites that steal your details. This guide shows you exactly how to identify these texts and what to do if you've already engaged with one.

item.title: WhatsApp 'New Number' Scam UK: How to Spot Family Impersonation Tricks
item.description: Scammers access or create WhatsApp accounts and message your contacts pretending to be a family member with a new phone number. They quickly build trust before asking for urgent financial help, often citing emergencies like hospital bills or legal problems.

item.title: Vinted scams UK: how to spot and avoid fake sellers and buyers
item.description: Vinted is a legitimate peer-to-peer marketplace, but scammers use fake seller and buyer accounts to steal money, personal details, or goods. This guide explains the most common Vinted fraud patterns and how to stay safe.

item.title: Crypto Scams UK: How to Spot and Avoid Investment Fraud
item.description: Crypto scams cost UK residents millions every year. Scammers use fake investment platforms, celebrity endorsements, and pressure tactics to steal your money and personal data. This guide shows you exactly how these scams work and what to do if you've been targeted.

item.title: Temu Scam: How to Spot Fake Offers and Protect Yourself in the UK
item.description: Scammers impersonate Temu to lure UK shoppers into fake cashback offers, money transfer schemes, and data harvesting. This guide shows you the real warning signs and what to do if you've been targeted.

item.title: Bank Transfer Scam UK: How to Spot and Stop Fraudulent Transfers
item.description: Bank transfer scams are among the most common fraud tactics in the UK, costing victims thousands of pounds. Scammers use fake messages, spoofed emails, and impersonation to convince you to transfer money urgently to accounts they control.

item.title: Tinder Scam UK: How to Spot Fake Profiles and Protect Yourself
item.description: Tinder scams are common in the UK, with fraudsters creating fake profiles to build relationships and extract money or personal information. This guide reveals exactly how these scams work and what to do if you suspect you're being targeted.

item.title: Debt Relief Scam UK: How to Spot and Avoid Fake Debt Help
item.description: Scammers pose as debt advisers or consolidation companies, promising to reduce or erase your debts in exchange for upfront fees. They target people in financial difficulty and often use pressure tactics and fake credentials to gain trust.

item.title: AliExpress Scam UK: How to Spot Fake Sellers and Protect Yourself
item.description: AliExpress scams typically involve fake sellers offering items at suspiciously low prices, taking payment, and either sending counterfeit goods or nothing at all. UK buyers are particularly targeted because of higher purchasing power, and scammers exploit AliExpress's dispute resolution system to avoid refunds.

item.title: Bitcoin Scam UK: How to Spot and Avoid Crypto Fraud
item.description: Bitcoin scams in the UK typically involve fraudsters posing as legitimate crypto platforms, investment advisors, or recovery services to steal money or personal data. This guide explains the most common Bitcoin scam patterns and how to protect yourself.

item.title: LinkedIn Job Scam: How to Spot Fake Recruitment Offers in the UK
item.description: Scammers create convincing LinkedIn profiles posing as recruiters or hiring managers, offering fake jobs to lure UK job seekers. They often ask for upfront fees, bank details, or personal information under the pretence of processing applications or background checks.

item.title: Microsoft Support Scam UK: How to Spot Fake Support Calls and Protect Your PC
item.description: Microsoft support scams trick UK residents into believing their computer has a virus or security problem, then trick them into handing over remote access or payment details. This guide shows you exactly how to spot the scam and what to do if you've already engaged with the scammers.

item.title: Facebook Marketplace Scam UK: How to Spot Fake Sellers and Protect Your Money
item.description: Facebook Marketplace scams cost UK buyers thousands every year through fake listings, fake payment systems, and non-delivery of goods. This guide shows you exactly how to verify sellers, spot red flags before you pay, and what to do if you've already sent money.

item.title: Job Offer Scam: How to Spot Fake Recruitment in the UK
item.description: Job offer scams trick people into sending money or sharing sensitive information by pretending to be legitimate employers or recruitment agencies. Scammers often target job seekers on LinkedIn, Indeed, and via email, offering unusually high pay with minimal qualifications required.

item.title: Facebook Marketplace Scams: How to Protect Yourself in the UK
item.description: Facebook Marketplace has become a popular platform for buying and selling items in the UK, but scammers are increasingly targeting unsuspecting users. This guide covers the most common scams, warning signs to watch for, and practical steps you can take to protect yourself when using the platform.

item.title: Evri Text Message Scam: How to Spot and Avoid It
item.description: Evri text scams trick UK residents with fake delivery notifications claiming packages need attention. These messages contain malicious links designed to steal personal and financial information. This guide explains how to recognise the scam, what to do if you receive one, and how to report it to authorities.

item.title: Gumtree Scams in the UK: How to Spot and Avoid Them
item.description: Gumtree remains one of the UK's largest online marketplaces, but scammers frequently target buyers and sellers. This guide covers common fraud tactics including fake listings, payment schemes, overpayment tricks, and advance-fee scams. Discover practical steps to verify sellers, secure transactions, and report suspicious activity to stay safe.

item.title: Is SHEIN Legit or a Scam? What UK Shoppers Need to Know
item.description: SHEIN operates as a legal business, but shoppers in the UK face real risks including delays of 2-4 weeks, inconsistent product quality, unexpected import fees, and limited consumer protections. This guide explains what to expect, how to spot problems, and your rights when ordering from SHEIN to the UK.

item.title: Gumtree Scams UK: How to Spot and Avoid Common Fraud Risks
item.description: Gumtree is one of the UK's largest classifieds platforms, but it attracts scammers targeting both buyers and sellers. This guide covers the most prevalent Gumtree scams, red flags to watch for, safe payment methods, and steps to take if you suspect fraudulent activity. Follow our advice to buy and sell safely.

item.title: eBay Buyer Protection in the UK: What You Need to Know to Stay Safe
item.description: This guide explains eBay's buyer protection programme for UK customers, including how to file claims for non-delivery and item not as described cases. Discover red flags to watch for, best practices when purchasing, and what to do if you fall victim to a scam on the platform.

item.title: Royal Mail Text Scam: How to Spot and Avoid Fraudulent SMS Messages
item.description: Royal Mail text scams are increasingly common in the UK, with criminals sending fake SMS messages claiming delivery issues or requiring payment. These messages contain malicious links designed to steal banking details, addresses, and personal information. This guide explains how to recognise these scams, what to do if you receive one, and how to report fraudulent messages to Royal Mail and authorities.

item.title: DPD Delivery Scam Text Messages: How to Spot and Avoid Them
item.description: DPD delivery scam texts are a common fraud tactic in the UK where criminals send SMS messages pretending to be from the parcel delivery company. These messages typically claim a delivery failed or ask you to confirm details, urging you to click a link. Clicking these links can lead to credential theft, malware installation, or financial fraud. This guide explains how to recognise fake DPD texts, what to do if you receive one, and how to stay safe when expecting deliveries.
